channel.title: Blog Posts on
channel.description: Recent content in Blog Posts on

item.title: Linux Kernel: Abusing an AAW and a Heap Overflow for LPE (blunder driver challenge)
item.description: Introduction # This blog will be about exploiting a Linux Kernel Driver by chaining a Heap Overflow bug with a ‘sandboxed’ Arbitrary Address Write (AAW) for Local Priviledge Escalation by overwriting core_pattern with most mitigations enabled.

item.title: Linux Remote Process Injection - (Injecting into a firefox process)
item.description: Introduction # This blog will cover Remote Process Injection in Linux; a common evasion technique popular in the Windows Malware world.

item.title: Linux Malware: Defense Evasion Techniques
item.description: Introduction # This post will cover some of the different techniques that can be employed to avoid malware detection in a Linux host.

item.title: Introduction to Linux Kernel Exploitation: Stack-Overflows
Linux Process Injection - (Simple ptrace injection)
item.description: Process Injection is a defense evasion technique that is often employed within malware and entails a method of executing arbitrary code in the address space of a separate live process, therefore, allowing access to the process’s memory, system resources and possibly network resources.

item.title: Sig-Return Oriented Programming Attack - (SROP)
item.description: This is a yet another binary exploitation attack that is somewhat similar to return-oriented-programming (ROP) and to understand how this works, we first need to understand how C signals work.

item.title: Pwnkit: Linux Privilege Escalation (CVE-2021-4034)
item.description: This was a Linux Priviledge Escalation (LPE) vulnerability in polkit’s pkexec that affected almost every linux distribution.
